current_task: Stabilize the Railway production deployment after adding login
completed: Copy and adapt agent documentation from the reference project
Create .NET 10 solution with Clean Architecture style layers
Implement JSON local persistence for projects, surveys, mentions and requirements
Create responsive static web UI with voice-capture support
Split RequirementWorkspaceService into focused AppCore services
Split the monolithic browser script into ES modules by feature
Add project guardrails for architecture and dual-theme UI support
Enable persistent dark mode for the web app
Document current system state, interview workflow and survey behavior agreements
Add LAN run/publish scripts for phone access
Add session login and Docker deployment prep
Fix production auth route grouping so login and workspace APIs work together
Expand default system template seeds for production
Regenerate minute drafts when only the header was saved
pending: Continue splitting the survey frontend module into smaller units
Decide whether to keep JSON or move to SQLite after real usage
Validate dictation behavior on a real mobile browser
Validate hosted login plus persistent volume on a real Internet deployment
Retest production project/template creation after Railway redeploy
Confirm the full default template pack appears in Railway
Retest production minute generation after a dictated interview
notes: The app has no login by design.
Personal data is stored in data/workspace.json and ignored by git.
Voice dictation uses the browser Web Speech API with es-MX language.
The API serves the static frontend from wwwroot.
The frontend now boots as ES modules from wwwroot/js.
The UI must render correctly in both light and dark themes.
Survey transcript and minute output should preserve chronological turn order per question.
Interview behavior and current implementation agreements are documented in docs/agent/current-system-state.md and docs/product/interview-workflow.md.
The recommended first mobile setup is LAN access from the same Wi-Fi network.
Internet deployment now expects auth secrets in environment variables and a persistent file path for workspace storage.
Auth endpoints must remain outside the protected workspace API route group.
Default system templates are seeded by name, so production can receive missing templates without duplicating existing ones.
Minute generation should regenerate title-only drafts when saved transcript turns exist.
decisions: Use JSON local storage for the first version instead of a database.
Serve a static frontend from the API to keep deployment simple.
Keep VABELRoutes-like layer boundaries, but avoid Mobile and authentication for this product.
Prefer focused AppCore helpers over one large orchestration service.
Prefer feature-based frontend modules over a single browser script file.
Prefer theme tokens over hardcoded colors for all new UI work.
Treat each speaker change as a new interview turn instead of merging all text by person within a question.
Require future survey/voice work to preserve the documented interview workflow unless the docs are intentionally updated.
Prefer LAN exposure over Internet deployment as the first phone-access path because the app is local-first and has no login.
Use a shared session login backed by environment variables as the first online protection layer instead of introducing a full user system.
Protect only workspace API endpoints with authorization; keep auth status/login/logout public.
next_actions: Use the app in a real interview and refine the capture flow.
Try the LAN script from a phone on the same Wi-Fi network.
Deploy the Docker image to a host with persistent disk and configure Auth__Password.
Break the remaining large survey module into capture, transcript and review submodules.
Add export to Markdown once the preferred requirement format stabilizes.
Keep current-system-state and interview-workflow documents in sync with future survey changes.
